q: 1,206 soldiers of an army have enough food for 24 days. If the number of soldiers in the army is increased by 1,809, then for how many days will the food last?
A certain principal becomes three times itself in 8 years at simple interest. In how many years will it become four times itself?
The volume of a cone is 34,650 cm^3. If its height is 25 cm, find the curved surface area of its base. (Use pi = 22/7)
A, B and C can do a piece of work in 2, 8 and 8 days respectively. If they all work together, in how much time (in days) will they complete the work?
A boat travels 36 km downstream in one hour, and the speed of the stream is 24 km/hr. The boat returns by the same route. Find the average speed (in km/hr) of the boat during the whole journey.

A solid spherical iron ball of radius 36 cm is dropped into a cylindrical vessel full of water, causing some water to overflow. If the radius of the base of the vessel is 8 metres, find the rise in water level (in metres) if all the water that overflowed is poured back.
Three numbers are in the ratio 7 : 20 : 8 and the sum of these numbers is 4830. Find the middle number.
A cricketer has an average of 55 runs over 5 innings. If he scores 166 runs in the 6th inning, by how many runs (rounded off to the nearest whole number) does his average score increase?
A trader buys 20 kg of apples for Rs.3,600 and sells them at the rate of Rs.220/kg. Find how much profit (in Rs.) he makes.
If 50% of 80% of 1/2 of a number is 442, find the number.
A person covers a certain distance at a speed of 55 km/hr and returns at a speed of 72 km/hr. If the total time taken for the entire journey is 3.5 hours, find the one-way distance (in km).

A shopkeeper declares the following three schemes of discounts. Which scheme is the most beneficial to a customer? A. Buy 5 items and get 2 of the same items free. B. Two successive discounts of 13% and 38%. C. Two successive equal discounts of 21%.

6 years ago, the ratio of the age of a daughter to her mother's age was 9 : 4. After 2 years, the ratio of the mother's age to the daughter's age will be 8 : 1. Find the present ratio of the mother's age to the daughter's age.
Simplify: (24 - 6 x 3) + 18 / 3 x 2
If 2a + b + c = 0 and 8a^3 + b^3 + c^3 = kabc, then find the value of k^2.
In triangle ABC, BD is perpendicular to AC at D and angle DBC = 72 degrees. E is a point on BC such that angle CAE = 71 degrees. What is the measure of angle AEB?

The cost of a washing machine is 20% less than the cost of a TV. If the cost of the washing machine increases by 45% and that of the TV decreases by 38%, then what is the percentage change in the total cost of 5 washing machines and 4 TVs?
7 men or 6 women can do a job in 15 days. 7 men work for 8 days and leave. Find the number of women required to complete the remaining work in 6 days.
If 12, 9, 10, 12, 13, (2K - 2), 13, 19, 15 and 17 have an average of 12, find the value of K.
The sum of two numbers is 25 and their product is 144. Of these, one number is 9 more than the other. Find the smaller number.
If tan A = 6/7, then find the value of (7 sin A + 2 cos A) / (7 sin A - 2 cos A).
During a shoe sale, 9 sweaters were bought for the price of 3 trousers. In this sale, what percentage discount was offered?

In the following number series, the numbers are arranged from left to right and the answers to the questions given are based on them (all numbers consist of only single digits). If each odd number is increased by 1 first and then each even number is decreased by 1: (Left) 1 8 1 5 3 8 5 2 5 2 3 3 7 4 4 5 2 7 8 7 2 (Right). How many such digits are there which are odd just before and odd just after such operation?

Five friends A, B, C, D and E are sitting in a row facing the same direction. A is sitting at the extreme right end and B is to the right of E. A and B are sitting at the extreme ends. D is sitting just to the left of A. Who is sitting just between A and D?
In a certain code, MH 10 is related to FA 20 and in the same way PK 9 is related to ID 18. By the same logic, to which is UP 7 related?

A person starts from point A and walks towards the east; after walking 54 km he turns right, then after walking 56 km he turns right again, then after walking 56 km he turns right again, then after walking 65 km he turns right again, then after walking 9 km he turns right and stops at point B which is 2 km from point A. In which direction and how far must he walk from point A to reach point B by the shortest route? (Note: All turns are only 90 degrees, and when a turn is mentioned it has been made.)

The numbers given below are based on three-digit numbers. (Left) 437 504 395 482 273 (Right). (Example: in 697, first digit = 6, second digit = 9 and third digit = 7.) Note: All numbers are arranged from left to right. If the largest number is added to the difference between the second-smallest digit of the smallest number and its third digit, what will be the result?

In the following number series, the numbers given are arranged from left to right and the answers given are based on them (all numbers consist of only single digits). (Left) 7 5 2 1 1 6 6 9 8 6 1 7 1 1 9 6 7 3 6 9 9 8 1 (Right). How many such odd digits are there which have an odd number just before and an odd number just after them?

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions is/are true. Statements: No Pianos are Guitars. All Guitars are Violins. Some Violins are Drums. Conclusions: (I) Some Pianos are Violins. (II) Some Guitars are Drums.
Nirav is sitting 16th from the top in his class and 26th from the bottom. How many students are there in the class?

Some statements regarding writers and singers are given. You have to consider these statements true even if they appear different from the commonly known facts and decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements. Statements: All singers are professors. All professors are doctors. Conclusions: (I) Some doctors are singers. (II) All singers are doctors.

In a certain code language, P + Q means P is the mother of Q; P / Q means P is the father of Q; P x Q means P is the brother of Q; and P - Q means P is the sister of Q. If C / B + A x V & G # K, then how is C related to K?

The numbers given below are based on three-digit numbers. (Left) 658 223 661 257 666 (Right). (Example: in 658, first digit = 6, second digit = 5 and third digit = 8.) Note: All numbers are arranged from left to right. If the largest digit of the largest number is added to the third digit of the smallest number, what will be the result?
If '+' means '-', '-' means 'x', 'x' means '/' and '/' means '+', then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation? 205 - 3 + 262 + 212 x 4 = ?
In a certain code language, 'ZEST' is written as '4875' and 'BEST' is written as '5781'. In that language, what is the code for 'Z'?
In a certain code language, 'TIME' is coded as '5046' and 'MINE' is coded as '4609'. In that language, what is the code for 'N'?
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following series? 56 76 96 116 136 ?
On the basis of the given pairs, LHQV is related to QFVT in a certain way. Similarly, VDAR is related to ABFP. If the same logic is followed, then to which of the following is FZKN related?
If '+' and '-' are interchanged and 'x' and '/' are interchanged, then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation? 208 - 117 x 9 + 5 x 215 = ?

Seven friends A, B, C, D, E and F are sitting in a row, all facing north. E is sitting to the right of A. G is sitting just between B and C. A is sitting just between D and E. E is sitting to the left of C. B is sitting to the right of F. Who is sitting second from the left end?
